title: 2026 Jacobian counterexample reproduction
statement: Reproduce the announced polynomial map in three variables, verify that its Jacobian determinant is the nonzero constant -2, and verify that three distinct rational points have the same image.
source_name: MathOverflow verification thread and cited announcement
verifiability: Exact symbolic identity and three rational witness evaluations
rationale: A small, decisive, independently reproducible calibration of the entire evidence pipeline. This is prior work, not a Proof Factory discovery.

title: Erdős–Straus conjecture
statement: For every integer n > 2, do there exist distinct integers 1 ≤ x < y < z such that 4/n = 1/x + 1/y + 1/z?
verifiability: A counterexample is finite; proposed parametric families and congruence covers can be checked exactly; the statement has a third-party Lean formalization.
rationale: Parked after two epochs in favor of a finite hard-lane exact-value target. Its candidate finite classification remains preserved for isolated reconstruction and author/priority review; it is not an Erdős–Straus solution.
parked_reason: Hard lane now prioritizes a finite exact problem with compact lower-bound witnesses and certificate-carrying upper-bound proofs.

title: Ramsey number R(5,5)
statement: Determine R(5,5), the least n such that every graph on n vertices contains either a 5-clique or an independent set of size 5. The current range is 43 <= R(5,5) <= 46. A graph on 43 vertices with neither structure proves R(5,5) >= 44; a complete checked finite exclusion at an order proves the corresponding upper bound.
source_name: Radziszowski, Small Ramsey Numbers, Dynamic Survey (2026)
verifiability: A lower-bound witness is a 43-vertex adjacency list independently checked by enumerating every 5-vertex subset for cliques and independent sets. Any upper-bound claim must include a deterministic CNF/case encoding, checked SAT/UNSAT leaf certificates, a checked exhaustive cube cover, and an independently verified generator-to-graph correspondence.
rationale: The flagship is famous but finite: it has a narrow 43-46 gap, a tiny decisive construction certificate, and a credible certificate-carrying SAT route for upper bounds. This replaces a universal conjecture whose bounded computation could not directly settle it.
external_channel: Small Ramsey Numbers Dynamic Survey; graph-theory and SAT/formal-verification venues
field_progress_gates: Find a zero-conflict K43 witness that passes both independent exact checkers.
Find an order-42 graph outside the supplied 656 isomorphism classes, with two exact graph-property checks, canonical isomorphism separation, and a current novelty review.
Lower the best known conflict count within a broader, independently interesting, precisely defined family.
Prove a concise structural theorem explaining why the two-orbit or near-circulant family cannot work.
Produce certified exclusions that materially shrink an accepted R(5,5) search space, with deterministic encodings, checked proof logs, and sound symmetry coverage.
Improve a search algorithm on held-out Ramsey benchmarks under matched compute, exact evaluation, leakage-safe splits, and appropriate prior-art baselines.

title: Exact covering number C(12,6,4)
statement: Determine the minimum number of 6-subsets of a 12-point set needed to cover every 4-subset. The maintained range is 40 <= C(12,6,4) <= 41; either a verified 40-block cover or a complete independently checked exclusion of 40 settles the exact value.
source_name: La Jolla Covering Repository, cross-checked against the maintained Covering Repository
verifiability: A positive result is a 40-block list checked directly against all 495 four-subsets. A negative result requires a deterministic symmetry-reduced encoding, an independently checked exhaustive cube/orbit cover, and replayable proof logs for every UNSAT leaf.
rationale: This is a meaningful one-bit exact design problem with 924 candidate blocks, strong forced point and pair multiplicities, a sound 10,395-fold perfect-matching quotient, compact positive certificates, and standard proof-producing SAT routes. It is the preferred easier-hard successor to R(5,5).
external_channel: La Jolla Covering Repository / Covering Repository; combinatorial-design and proof-computation venues
probability_basis: The current proof route independently classifies ordinary 20-block C(11,5,3) covers to discharge the unavailable Mills-uniqueness proof. The ordinary ledger has 32,611 replay-certified fifth leaves, 8,476 audited semantic zero-child contradictions, and 216 of 384 fourth-level parents independently aggregated closed. A cross-host-audited multi-deficit pilot now supplies a substantially smaller exact residual partition, but no new leaf or parent closure.
exact_resolution_probability_basis: The global extension ledger remains 33/47 certified and the ordinary-link classification is incomplete. On 12 frozen timed-out formulas, the exact multi-deficit pilot reduced 3,714 generic next branches to 234 exact first-step branches (93.6995%) and 1,015,494 naive two-block literal extensions to 4,402 exhaustive depth-two frontier cubes (99.5665%). Both local and cloud audits pass, but the pilot found zero semantic contradictions and therefore creates no closure.
phase0_evidence: Current certified bound is 40 <= C(12,6,4) <= 41 and the global extension ledger is 33/47. Ordinary classification remains at 32,611 replay-certified fifth leaves, 8,476 audited zero-child contradictions, and 216/384 aggregation-closed fourth parents. A deterministic 12-formula paired multi-deficit gate covers both roots, rank-zero/q3/q4, and first/last second-orbit positions. It replaces 3,714 generic next branches with 234 exact residual-orbit branches and 1,015,494 naive two-block literal extensions with 4,402 exhaustive depth-two frontier cubes. Independent local and cloud audits pass; zero semantic contradiction or SAT candidate occurred, so no closure is claimed.
manual_scope: Continuously operate the checkpointed C(12,6,4) campaign through bounded canonical-frontier and materially different propagation/cardinality discriminators. Preserve independent reconstruction and proof replay, serialize heavy work on the current host, pause at declared information-value gates, provision no paid burst capacity, and make no exact-value claim before complete coverage and a second cardinality validation route.

research_authorization.scope: Run the evidence-gated, checkpointed C(12,6,4) campaign continuously under existing CPU, memory, disk, correctness, and information-value limits. Completed artifacts remain durable until reviewed; no paid scaling or public mathematical claim is authorized.

title: A divisor-function record beyond 24
statement: Let τ(n) count the positive divisors of n. Is there some n > 24 such that max over m < n of (m + τ(m)) is at most n + 2?
verifiability: A positive answer is one explicit integer plus a bounded exact divisor-count check.
rationale: The first genuine discovery target: a short statement with a compact deterministic witness and a natural optimized-search harness.

title: Prime reciprocal product sets
statement: Are there finite sets of primes P and Q such that 1 = (sum over p in P of 1/p)(sum over q in Q of 1/q)?
verifiability: A positive answer is a pair of finite prime sets checked by exact rational arithmetic.
rationale: Finite certificates are decisive, though the known lower bound of 60 total primes makes search engineering nontrivial.

title: Johnson graph coloring witness
statement: Does some k > 2 admit a coloring of the k-subsets of {1,…,2k} with k+1 colors such that every (k+1)-subset sees all k+1 colors?
verifiability: A coloring is a finite certificate; SAT/CP-SAT encodings can be independently checked.
rationale: A clean finite combinatorics search with reusable SAT certificates; known false for 3 ≤ k ≤ 8 narrows the first unknown cases.

title: Distinct odd covering system
statement: Does there exist a distinct covering system all of whose moduli are odd?
verifiability: An explicit finite covering system can be checked over one least-common-multiple period.
rationale: A finite witness would settle the existence direction, but structural lower bounds make it a later discovery-lane target.

title: Erdős problem #1041
statement: Let $f(z)=\prod_{i=1}^n(z-z_i)\in \mathbb{C}[z]$ with $\lvert z_i\rvert < 1$ for all $i$. Must there always exist a path of length less than $2$ in\[\{z: \lvert f(z)\rvert < 1\}\]which connects two of the roots of $f$?
rationale: Added from the versioned Erdős Problems community database to keep the discovery frontier broad. The first pass must validate the exact statement, status, literature, and a concrete verification contract.
verifiability: Official database status is falsifiable; refine the exact certificate contract before any candidate claim.

Its stated building blocks already exist in Mathlib, making it a smaller and clearer first accepted contribution than the finite-set-over-reals API required for Erdős #530.

title: Lean formalization of Erdős Problem #530 (largest guaranteed Sidon subset)
statement: Add a scoped Lean declaration for: letting ℓ(N) be the largest k such that every finite A ⊆ ℝ with |A| = N has a Sidon subset S ⊆ A of size k, conjecturally ℓ(N) ~ √N. The contribution is the precise formal statement and supporting definitions, not a claimed proof.
source_name: Erdős Problems (Thomas F. Bloom, maintained database)
verifiability: A self-contained Lean file defining the Sidon predicate and extremal function, with the conjecture declaration typechecked by Lean’s kernel; CI and an independent local `lake build` reproduce the check.
rationale: The maintained page was edited 8 April 2026, labels the problem OPEN, says it is not formalised, and records no one currently formalising it. The linked formal-conjectures issue is open, explicitly up for grabs, unassigned, and has no branch or PR. This is unusually small because it is one exact research-conjecture interface with finite-set and additive-equality primitives already standard in Lean, yet it contributes a reusable machine-checkable target without pretending to solve the conjecture.

title: Formalize that products of strict continuous group homomorphisms are strict
statement: In Mathlib, prove that if f : G₁ →* H₁ and g : G₂ →* H₂ are continuous, topologically strict group homomorphisms, then Prod.map f g is strict; add the narrowly required API expressing strictness through rangeRestrict/kernel quotient and the existing IsOpenQuotientMap product theorem.
verifiability: A Lean theorem with no axioms or sorrys, compiled by Mathlib CI; the issue identifies the existing lemmas IsOpenQuotientMap.prodMap and Homeomorph.Set.prod that provide an independently checkable proof route.
rationale: This is explicitly labeled a good first issue, has a concrete proof architecture from a Mathlib maintainer, and is an unusually bounded formalization gap in topological-group infrastructure rather than a speculative open-ended theorem hunt.

title: Correct the transformer-block LayerNorm parameter count in DeepMind AI Foundations
statement: Fix both reference implementations for Course 4 Lab 4.5 so a transformer block counts two LayerNorm components, each with a trainable gamma and beta vector: use `2 * parameter_count_layer_norm(hyperparams)` (equivalently `4 * embedding_dim`) instead of `2 * embedding_dim`, and add or update a focused regression check.
verifiability: The notebook and packaged feedback reference must agree that one LayerNorm owns gamma and beta vectors of length `embedding_dim` and that the block constructs two LayerNorms. Test several embedding sizes and assert `block = attention + MLP + 2 * LayerNorm` in both copies.
rationale: This is a fresh, exact arithmetic error in official DeepMind teaching material. Current main defines each LayerNorm as two trainable vectors and constructs two such layers, but both answer implementations count only one layer's two vectors. The repair is tiny, educationally meaningful, and has a deterministic test oracle.

title: Preserve valid HMM marginals for padded sequences in Distrax
statement: Fix `HMM.backward` so padded suffix scan steps preserve `beta_prev` rather than replacing the backward carry with zeros, then add a regression proving that `forward_backward(obs, length=L)` matches `forward_backward(obs[:L])` on the valid prefix and log-likelihood.
verifiability: Run the issue's deterministic two-state HMM reproduction and compare padded versus sliced observations for the first L alpha, beta, and posterior values plus log-likelihood. Add edge cases for full length and a one-step valid prefix and run the focused HMM test suite.
rationale: Current main explicitly substitutes a zero vector when the reverse scan is in padding, which then propagates into valid timesteps. The issue supplies a minimal reproduction, the corrective carry behavior is local, and the regression oracle is equality with the unpadded prefix.

repository_recognition.evidence: Official Google DeepMind JAX probability-distributions library with 644 GitHub stars and current repository activity, checked 2026-07-22.

upstream_work_check.evidence: The issue has zero comments and no assignee. All 30 open PRs were searched and reviewed for HMM, backward, padding, or issue #337; none matches, and the reporter has no matching PR.

campaign_last_assessment.close_signal: The one-line patch, repository regression, pristine failure, patched replays, focused suite, independent six-profile checker, and hash-bound completion receipt are all present.
campaign_last_assessment.reason: The local technical contribution is candidate-complete; one bounded continuation for human review and upstream CI has high value, while further unsourced parameter sweeps do not.

title: Fix SynthID Text's inverted GPU training guard
statement: In `BayesianDetector.train_best_detector`, reject unsupported CPU training by changing the device condition from `torch_device.type in ("cuda", "tpu")` to `not in`, and add focused CPU-reject and accelerator-accept regression tests without requiring physical accelerator hardware.
verifiability: Mock the downstream training work and assert that a CPU device raises the documented error while CUDA and TPU device types pass the guard. Run the detector's focused tests and verify the condition agrees with its error message.
rationale: Current main raises the CPU-instability error precisely when the caller supplies CUDA or TPU. This one-line logical inversion blocks the documented supported execution path; the behavior can be covered with small branch tests and no accelerator dependency.

repository_recognition.evidence: Official Google DeepMind SynthID text-watermarking repository with 929 GitHub stars and current maintainer activity, checked 2026-07-22.

upstream_work_check.evidence: The issue has zero comments and no assignee. Current main still contains the inverted guard; all four open PRs and the reporter's open repository PRs were checked and none addresses it.

campaign_last_assessment.close_signal: The hash-bound patch applies to unchanged live main, focused tests pass 3/3, and both pristine mutation control and independent checker pass.
campaign_last_assessment.reason: The bounded contribution is candidate-complete; continuation means isolated human review and upstream validation, not another local branch replay.

title: Implement AlphaGeometry's missing two-circle tangent construction
statement: Add the missing numerical implementation for the declared `cc_tangent0` construction, reusing the established four-point `sketch_cc_tangent` geometry where appropriate, and add equal-radius and unequal-radius tests that validate the two returned contact points.
verifiability: Exercise numerical dispatch for `cc_tangent0` and assert it no longer raises a missing-function KeyError. For equal and unequal radii, verify each returned point lies on its respective circle and the line through the points is perpendicular to both radius vectors, then run the existing numerical geometry tests.
rationale: AlphaGeometry's definitions file exposes `cc_tangent0`, but numerical dispatch requires a same-named `sketch_` function and current main provides only `sketch_cc_tangent`. The missing bounded construction is a real hole in a high-profile mathematical reasoning system and the geometric invariants give an independent oracle.

repository_recognition.evidence: Official Google DeepMind AlphaGeometry repository for olympiad geometry with 4,875 GitHub stars, checked 2026-07-22.

upstream_work_check.evidence: The issue has zero comments and no assignee. Current main still lacks `sketch_cc_tangent0`; all 15 open PRs were checked and none implements or references the missing construction.
